title: Cosmiza coccinea
intro: Cosmiza coccinea, also known as Scarlet Plume Grass, is a type of flowering plant native to South America, characterized by its vibrant red flowers and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cosmiza coccinea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cosmiza coccinea is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to utilize its therapeutic benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
taxonomy: Cosmiza coccinea is a species of cicada in the family Cicadidae. It belongs to the genus Cosmiza, which is a group of cicadas that are typically found in Asia. The classification of Cosmiza coccinea is as follows: kingdom Animalia, phylum Arthropoda, class Insecta, order Hemiptera, family Cicadidae, genus Cosmiza, species C. coccinea. The family Cicadidae is divided into several subfamilies, including the Cicadinae and the Tibicininae. Further research is needed to determine the exact relationships between Cosmiza coccinea and other species within the family Cicadidae.
uses: Cosmiza coccinea, also known as the red-winged grasshopper, is primarily found in New Guinea and parts of Asia.
